text:
• What Happens When Overload Triggers
BEFORE OVERLOAD
ingressRequestRateLimiterEnabled = false
→ All operations admitted (no limit)
OVERLOAD DETECTED (tick N)
"Overload detected,
calculating rate limit" (ID: 45709)
2. Algorithm initializes with init_rate_per
3. setParameter: ingressRequestAdmissionRateper 1e.= 10000
10,000 ops/sec)
4. setParameter: ingressRequestAdmissionBurstCapacitySecs = 1.5
5. setParameter: ingressRequestRateLimiterEnabled = true
"Rate limit set" (ID: 45702)
7. Alert: "Atlas is actively regulating..." (ID: 44406)
CONTINUOUS TUNING (tick N+1, N+2, ...)
Each tick:
1. Measure current dwell_time
2. Compare to target_dwell_time (e.g., 5,000 us)
3. Adjust rate:
• dwell > target → DECREASE rate (still overloaded,
• dwell < target → INCREASE rate (recoverina)
- dwell = target → hold steady
4. setParameter: ingressRequestAdmissionRatePerSec = new_rate
OVERLOAD CLEARS
1. Signals drop below thresholds
2. Wait hold_duration_secs (default: 0)
setParameter: ingressRequestRateLimiterEnabled = false
"Rate limit disabled" (ID: 45705)
"Atlas is no longer regulating..." (ID: 44407)
6. Reset algorithm state
Rate Calculation (P-Controller Example)
Not arbitrary - it's a feedback loop targeting a specific dwell time:
init_rate_per_sec: 10000
- starting point when limiting begins
target_dwell_time_micros: 5000 - goal: 5ms queue wait
gain: 100
- sensitivity
Tick N (overload detected):
current_dwell = 50,000 us (way over target)
→ Start at init_rate: 10,000 ops/sec
Tick N+1:
current_dwell = 45,000 us (still high)
ratio = l0g10(45001 / 5001) = 0.95
change = 100 * 0.95 = 95 (capped at 25% = 2500)
new_rate = 10000 - 95 = 9905 ops/sec
Tick N+2:
current_dwell = 40,000 us (improving but still high)
→ rate decreases further
... continues until dwell = target...
Tick N+20:
current_dwell = 5,500 us (close to target)
ratio = log10(5501 / 5001) = 0.04
change = 100 * 0.04 = 4
new_rate = 7500 - 4 = 7496 ops/sec (small adjustment)
Tick N+30:
current_dwell = 4,000 us (below target - recovering!)
ratio = log10(4001 / 5001) = -0.10
change = 100 * -0.10 = -10
new_rate = 7496 - (-10) = 7506 ops/sec (rate INCREASES)
